rulings.Case Title: JAMES D. BANKS VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, Sep. 03, 2021, line 1. 1-CROSS DEFENDANT JAMES BANKS' DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT. Outstanding discovery order compliance is stayed until September 10, 2021. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, Sep. 03, 2021, line 4. DEFENDANT MICHAEL FRANKLIN's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that a 3 Day Notice is insufficient as to unauthorized occupants holding over after tenant had vacated. Defendants also fail to cite authority for the proposition that TPA 2019 is applicable to the properties already subject to SFRO or that Plaintiff has to allege that unauthorized occupants (as opposed to tenants) are or are not entitled to the protections of TPA 2019. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)
